message: bd-2z9p: activate wide-char overflow regression test

 {"id":"bd-2xsi","title":"demo_showcase: observability + debugging scenes","description":"Exercise RichLogger (+ RichTracingLayer), Pretty/Inspect, and Traceback in a believable error/debug workflow.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:27.657814798Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:48.281869762Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:59:48.281795464Z","close_reason":"All child scenes implemented: Traceback (bd-3fqt), RichLogger (bd-2rxj), controlled failure (bd-2wxz), Pretty/Inspect (bd-1365), tracing (bd-325x). Observability and debugging showcase complete.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3g9v","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5v","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":43,"issue_id":"bd-2xsi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Story beat

After the dashboard “run” starts, introduce a controlled failure (e.g. service returns 500 / config invalid) and show how rich_rust helps debug:

- **Logging**: RichLogger formatted lines, keyword highlighting, hyperlinks to file paths
- **Tracing** (feature `tracing`): span/event demo integrated into the same output stream
- **Pretty/Inspect**: show the request/config struct being inspected
- **Traceback**: print a Rich-style exception traceback with synthetic frames

## Acceptance criteria

- Output is clearly more readable than raw logs (level colors, timestamps, structured hints).
- If `tracing` feature is not enabled, the tracing scene prints a small “disabled” message and continues.
- Traceback output is visually distinct and uses theming/styling consistent with the demo palette.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:36:38Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2y5j","title":"E2E: Markdown rendering (feature: markdown)","description":"End-to-end Markdown tests (requires markdown feature): (1) Headers, (2) Lists, (3) Code blocks, (4) Links, (5) Tables, (6) Blockquotes, (7) Inline formatting, (8) Complex documents. Verify styled output.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"PinkDesert","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:49.282909092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T22:22:41.095551798Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T22:22:41.095488360Z","close_reason":"Created 53 E2E tests in tests/e2e_markdown_rendering.rs covering: headers (H1-H6), unordered/ordered/nested/task lists, code blocks (inline/fenced), links (with/without URL display), tables (borders, alignment, Unicode), blockquotes, inline formatting (bold/italic/strikethrough), complex documents, horizontal rules, style customization, edge cases (empty, whitespace, Unicode, narrow/wide width). All pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2y5j","depends_on_id":"bd-20dk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2y5j","depends_on_id":"bd-vh8c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2yy4","title":"Unit tests for layout.rs","description":"Comprehensive tests for src/renderables/layout.rs. Current: 2 tests. Target: 15+ tests covering:
(1) Layout creation (horizontal, vertical splits)
(2) LayoutSplitter methods (split_column, split_row)
(3) Region sizing (fixed, ratio, minimum)
(4) Nested layouts (2-3 levels deep)
(5) Content rendering within regions
(6) Edge cases (zero width, single region)
(7) Integration with Panel and Table
Test actual rendered output, verify segment positions match expected regions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PearlElk","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:53:17.946921653Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T19:41:26.352404843Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T19:41:26.352340453Z","close_reason":"Added 40 comprehensive tests for layout.rs: Region (3), LayoutSplitter (1), Layout creation (7), splitting (5), lookup (6), visibility (2), update (2), ratio/sizing (5), rendering (5), edge cases (4), integration (1), divide helpers (2). Total: 42 tests.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2yy4","depends_on_id":"bd-2sfj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2z9p","title":"Table: prevent wide-char overflow in narrow fixed-width columns","description":"Repro: tests/repro_wide_char_overflow.rs fails when a 2-cell CJK glyph is rendered in column width=1. Header/border width becomes 5 while row width becomes 6. Root cause is rendered row segments not being hard-clamped to resolved column width after text wrapping/styling. Fix by enforcing cell line width to exact column width in render_row_content and promote repro test from ignored to active.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":75,"created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:11.838487346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:16.562515747Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","table","unicode","width"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2z9p","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:11.865308177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
+{"id":"bd-2z9p","title":"Table: prevent wide-char overflow in narrow fixed-width columns","description":"Repro: tests/repro_wide_char_overflow.rs fails when a 2-cell CJK glyph is rendered in column width=1. Header/border width becomes 5 while row width becomes 6. Root cause is rendered row segments not being hard-clamped to resolved column width after text wrapping/styling. Fix by enforcing cell line width to exact column width in render_row_content and promote repro test from ignored to active.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":75,"created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:11.838487346Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:51.315592167Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:31:51.315531043Z","close_reason":"Completed: wide-char overflow fixed and repro promoted to active test","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["parity","table","unicode","width"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2z9p","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T16:28:11.865308177Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2zpy","title":"JSON parity: match Python Rich rich.json.JSON options + constructors","description":"Python Rich JSON supports constructing from JSON string with options: indent (int/str), highlight, skip_keys, ensure_ascii, check_circular, allow_nan, default, sort_keys, plus JSON.from_data. Bring rich_rust Json closer: define a JsonOptions struct, support Json::from_str_with_options, ensure_ascii behavior, sorting, and plain vs styled output parity. Add conformance fixtures/tests for these knobs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:51:20.583177885Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:32:52.314471651Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:32:52.314408173Z","close_reason":"Completed JSON parity: indent None/int/str, compact mode, tab expansion, ensure_ascii (UTF-16 surrogates), sort_keys/highlight options, from_data; conformance fixtures + e2e tests added; JSON now wraps at console width like Python Rich.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["json","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-2zqu","title":"Create TESTING.md documentation","description":"Create comprehensive TESTING.md guide covering:
(1) Test organization (unit vs integration vs e2e)
(2) Running tests (cargo test, specific modules)
(3) Using verbose logging in tests
(4) Test fixtures and harness usage
(5) Writing new tests (patterns and examples)
(6) Snapshot testing with insta
(7) Property testing with proptest
(8) Coverage reporting commands
Include examples for each section.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:55:15.174003272Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T23:04:12.202299474Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T23:04:12.202237348Z","close_reason":"TESTING.md already exists with comprehensive documentation covering all items in the task description","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2zqu","depends_on_id":"bd-md7f","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-313x","title":"demo_showcase: typography + spacing patterns","description":"Establish consistent spacing/typography patterns (rules, titles, padding) used everywhere.","notes":"Created typography.rs module with: section_header(), scene_header(), hint(), spacer(), divider(), status_badge(), status_text(), brand_accent(), muted() helpers plus SECTION_SPACING, PANEL_PADDING, BLOCK_MARGIN constants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:05.878118586Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T00:41:34.395344068Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T00:41:34.395219205Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","typography"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-313x","depends_on_id":"bd-1by8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-313x","depends_on_id":"bd-2fl5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-09T23:55:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"bd-313x","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Consistency rules

- Every scene starts with a styled section header (Rule + title).
- Use consistent padding around major panels.
- Prefer `Align` utilities for hero/title blocks.
- Avoid dense walls of text; break with rules, spacing, and small tables.

This bead exists to keep the final output feeling designed.
","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:40:33Z"}]}
